subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given the quadratic equation

$$ z^2+4z-(1+12i)=0 $$

let its roots be $z_1$ and $z_2$.

We need to find

$$ |z_1|^2+|z_2|^2 $$

Using Vieta’s formulas for

$$ az^2+bz+c=0, $$

we have

$$ z_1+z_2=-\frac{b}{a}=-4 $$

and

$$ z_1z_2=\frac{c}{a}=-(1+12i)=-1-12i $$

Now solve the equation to identify the roots.

$$ z^2+4z-(1+12i)=0 $$

Using the quadratic formula,

$$ z=\frac{-4\pm\sqrt{16+4(1+12i)}}{2} $$

$$ z=\frac{-4\pm\sqrt{20+48i}}{2} $$

Now observe that

$$ (6+4i)^2=36+48i-16=20+48i $$

So,

$$ \sqrt{20+48i}=6+4i $$

Hence the roots are

$$ z_1=\frac{-4+(6+4i)}{2}=1+2i $$

and

$$ z_2=\frac{-4-(6+4i)}{2}=-5-2i $$

Now compute their moduli squared:

$$ |z_1|^2=|1+2i|^2=1^2+2^2=5 $$

$$ |z_2|^2=|-5-2i|^2=(-5)^2+(-2)^2=25+4=29 $$

Therefore,

$$ |z_1|^2+|z_2|^2=5+29=34 $$

So the correct answer is

$$ \boxed{34} $$

Hence, Option D is correct.
title: JEE Main 2026 (Online) 5th April Evening Shift

subject: Mathematics
solution:
$$3 + 2\sqrt { - 54} $$

$$ = 9 - 6 + 2\sqrt { - 54} $$

$$ = 9 + {\left( {\sqrt 6 i} \right)^2} + 2.3.\sqrt 6 i$$

$$ = {3^2} + {\left( {\sqrt 6 i} \right)^2} + 2.3.\sqrt 6 i$$

$$ = {\left( {3 + \sqrt 6 i} \right)^2}$$

Similarly, $$\left( {3 - 2\sqrt { - 54} } \right) = {\left( {3 - \sqrt 6 i} \right)^2}$$

$$ \therefore {\left( {3 + 2\sqrt { - 54} } \right)^{{1 \over 2}}} - {\left( {3 - 2\sqrt { - 54} } \right)^{{1 \over 2}}}$$

$$ = \pm \left( {3 + \sqrt 6 i} \right) - \left[ { \pm \left( {3 - \sqrt 6 i} \right)} \right]$$

$$ = 6, - 6,2\sqrt 6 i, - 2\sqrt 6 i$$

$$ \therefore $$ Possible imaginary parts are $$2\sqrt 6 i, - 2\sqrt 6 i$$
